docs.title: Welcome to the Unofficial UdS (Students) Bioinformatics Website
docs.text:
Disclaimer: we are not affiliated with UdS and anything written here is for entertainment/educational purposes. All opinions are our own. Please refer to official sources if unsure.

The goal for this website is to provide resources for administrative related items in the bioinformatics department at UdS. No code or sensitive material will be shared here. Possibly study strategies and general advice for courses.

Made by students, for students.

*please mind that this is a rough draft, major changes are still to be made.

docs.title: Adding to the Website
docs.text:
In order to add to the website:

  • Fork this project
  • Add .md file you would like to add into the docs folder
  • Additionally, you must follow the specific syntax as follows:
    • simple page: - Adding to the Website: add-website.md)
    • dropdown: you must have this nested system (refer to the example below)
- nav:
  - Home: index.md
  [...]
  - Technical:
      - Logging into SSH: ssh.md
  - Adding to the Website: add-website.md)

docs.title: When to Ask for Help
docs.text:
When faced with a question, please use this workflow:

  1. Check LSF/CMS/Teams accordingly.
  2. Write in forum of classroom - TAs are there to help you.
  3. Ask Bioinformatics Whatsapp Group chat.
  4. If not answered directly, then finally email the professor.
  5. We recommend you to check the Emailing Professors page

docs.title: Contribution Credits
docs.text:
This project is a collaborative effort by the UdS students Bioinformatics community. Our goal is to ship high-quality, student-led documentation to help everyone navigate the program more effectively.

Below is the official contributions of our volunteers. These serve as verification for professional portfolios.

docs.title: Project Founder & Maintainer
docs.text: Ben Horvath * Role: Lead Developer & Repository Owner. * Contributions: Initial site architecture (MkDocs), aesthetic design, automation via GitHub Actions, and final editorial review of all content.

docs.title: Core Contributors
docs.text: Contributor Areas of Contribution Key Deliverables Name/GitHub Handle e.g., Lab Index, Toolkit e.g., Drafted MPI-INF lab profiles, UV setup guide. Name/GitHub Handle e.g., Admin Guide e.g., Mapped LSF elective registration process. Name/GitHub Handle e.g., Course Catalog e.g., Detailed reviews for Advanced Molecular Genetics.

docs.title: Technical & Peer Review
docs.text:
Special thanks to those who helped refine the guide through bug reports, technical suggestions, or proofreading.

  • [Name/GitHub]: Identified and fixed broken links in the International Support section.
  • [Name/GitHub]: Provided peer review for the Math-heavy module resources.

docs.title: How to get listed 🤝
docs.text:
1. Reserve a Topic: Check the pinned Google Doc in our Slack channel to claim a section. (https://docs.google.com/document/d/1EK7aFrO-XtS7AoqucHEm8m9LSBD49zq36NQMc7X9p04/edit?usp=sharing)
  2. Submit a PR: Contribute high-quality Markdown content to the repository.
  3. Get Approved: Once your Pull Request is merged by the maintainer, your name and specific contributions will be added to this page.

This guide is "unofficial" and community-maintained. We thank all our contributors for keeping the information accurate and up-to-date.

docs.title: Emailing Professors
docs.text: *inspiration from Gemini v3

docs.title: Email Etiquette: Bioinformatics & Computational Biology
docs.text: In the German interdisciplinary landscape, Bioinformatics sits at the intersection of the Life Sciences and Informatics. This guide covers the formal requirements of German academia while addressing the technical specifics of computational research.

docs.title: 1. The Subject Line (Präzision ist Alles)
docs.text:
In Bioinformatics, professors often juggle lab management, teaching, and dry-lab research. Your subject line must include the context (Course, Lab, or Server) and the specific technical issue.

  • Format: [Module/Project Name] – [Specific Technical Issue/Question] – [Name]
  • Example (Course): Bioinformatics II (Modul 567) – Bug in Sequence Alignment Script – Jane Doe
  • Example (Research/Thesis): Master Thesis – RNA-Seq Pipeline Optimization – Max Mustermann

docs.title: 2. The Salutation (Titel und Hierarchie)
docs.text:
Even in "tech-heavy" labs, do not drop the formal titles unless explicitly told to do so.

Role Correct Salutation Professor / Lab Head Sehr geehrte Frau Prof. Dr. [LastName] PostDoc / Senior Researcher Sehr geehrter Herr Dr. [LastName] PhD Student / TA Hallo [FirstName/LastName] (Usually acceptable in lab settings, but use Sehr geehrte(r) for the first contact)

docs.title: 3. Communication Style: The "Dry-Lab" Approach
docs.text:
German communication is direct. In Bioinformatics, this means providing enough data for the recipient to understand the problem without having to ask follow-up questions.

  • The Problem Description: If you are reporting a bug or a pipeline error, don't just say "it doesn't work." State the tool, the environment (e.g., Conda, Docker, HPC), and the error message.
  • The "Sie" Factor: Always use the formal Sie in your initial correspondence.
  • Code Snippets: Never paste 100 lines of code into the body of an email. Use a link to a private GitHub Gist, a GitLab snippet, or attach a minimal reproducible example.

docs.title: A. Requesting HPC/Server Access
docs.text: When emailing a System Administrator or a Professor for cluster access, be professional and provide your technical "credentials" immediately. * Include: Your SSH public key (if requested), your Matrikelnummer, and the specific project/lab you are associated with.

docs.title: B. Troubleshooting Assistance
docs.text: Before emailing a Professor or TA about a coding issue, ensure you have checked the documentation. * The Phrase: "Ich habe bereits die Dokumentation von [Tool Name] geprüft, konnte jedoch keine Lösung für das Problem [Error] finden." (I have checked the documentation but couldn't find a solution...)

docs.title: 5. The Sign-off & Signature
docs.text:
Keep it standard.

  • Standard: Mit freundlichen Grüßen
[Your Full Name]
M.Sc. Bioinformatics Student
Matrikelnummer: [1234567]
[Optional: GitHub Profile Link]

docs.title: LSF vs CMS
docs.text:
• Use LSF to ensure you are legally registered for the exam so your ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) points actually count.
  • Use CMS/MS Teams to hear about announcements, complete assignments, learn about tutorials and interact with the Teaching Assistants (TAs).
Feature LSF / HIS / POS (Administrative) CMS / Moodle / LMS (Operational) Primary Purpose Official legal records and enrollment. Daily learning and course management. Course Catalog Master list of every course offered. Specific portal for your active courses. Registration Exam registration (Legal requirement). Course participation (Access to files). Course Materials None (Metadata/Descriptions only). Lecture slides, scripts, and datasets. Submissions None (Only final grades are viewed here). Uploading homework, code, and reports. Bioinformatics Booking lab slots or module credit info. Accessing Conda environments or server info.

docs.text:

docs.title: Logging into SSH
docs.text:
Add the following in your ssh:

  • Mac/Linux: nano ~/.ssh/config
  • Windows: notepad $HOME.ssh\config

There are computers from 1-XX(~24) available.

Host bioXX.studcs.uni-saarland.de
        HostName bioXX.studcs.uni-saarland.de
        User abcd1234
